volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Braggart Soldier (Section 4.2.1065 - Section 4.3.1123)

translation.label: Scaife English translation
translation.citation: Miles Gloriosus, or The Braggart Captain

sections.label: Section 4.2.1065
sections.original: tum argénti montes , nón massas habet , Aetna mons non aeque altos . †
sections.translation: Then , of silver , he has mountains , not ingots ; Aetna is not so high .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1066
sections.original: Eu ecástor hominem périurum .
sections.translation: By the stars ! O , what a lie !

sections.label: Section 4.2.1066b
sections.original: Vt ludó ?
sections.translation: How rarely I am playing him off !

sections.label: Section 4.2.1066c
sections.original: Quid ego ? ut sublécto ?
sections.translation: And I ; how do I do it ? Ain ’ t I gulling him ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.1067
sections.original: sed amábo , mitte me áctutum .
sections.translation: But , prithee , do let me go now .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1067b
sections.original: Quin tu huíc respondes áliquid ,
sections.translation: But do you give her some answer , either that you will do it , or that you won ’ t do it .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1068b
sections.original: Quid illám miseram animi excrúcias ,
sections.translation: Why cause this poor lady so much anguish of mind , who has never deserved any ill of you ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.1069b
sections.original: Iube eámpse exire huc ád nos .
sections.translation: Bid her come to me herself . Tell her that I will do everything that she requires .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1070b
sections.original: Facis núnc ut te facere aéquom ,
sections.translation: You now act as it is proper for you to act . since you wish the same yourself that she is wishing .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1071b
sections.original: Non ínsulsum huic ingénium .
sections.translation: No poor faculty of invention has she .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1072
sections.original: Quomque me óratricem haud sprévisti sistíque exorare éx te .
sections.translation: Since too you have not scouted your petitioner , and have suffered me to prevail upon you . ( Aside to PALAESTRIO .) How now ? Haven ’ t I played him off ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.1073b
sections.original: Nequeo hércle equidem risú meo moderari .
sections.translation: Faith , I couldn ’ t refrain from laughing .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1073c
sections.translation: Yes ; and for the same reason I turned in this direction away from you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1074b
sections.original: Non édepol tu scis , múlier ,
sections.translation: By my troth , wench , you don ’ t understand

sections.label: Section 4.2.1075
sections.original: quantum égo honorem nunc ílli habeo .
sections.translation: how great an honor I am now paying her .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1075b
sections.original: Scio , et ístuc illi dícam .
sections.translation: I know , and I shall tell her so .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1076
sections.original: Contra aúro alii hanc vendére potuit operám .
sections.translation: To another he could have sold his favours for his weight in gold .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1076b
sections.original: Pol istuc tibi crédo .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I believe you in that .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1077
sections.original: Meri béllatores gígnuntur , quas híc praegnatis fécit ,
sections.translation: Of those that are parents by him true warriors are born , and his sons live eight hundred years .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1078b
sections.original: Vae tibi , nugátor .
sections.translation: Fie on you for a fibber !

sections.label: Section 4.2.1079
sections.original: Quin mílle annorum pérpetuo vivónt , ab saeclo ad saéclum .
sections.translation: Why , straight on , from age to age , they live for a thousand years .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1080
sections.original: Eo mínus dixi , ne haec cénseret me advórsum se mentíri .
sections.translation: I spoke within limits , for the reason that she mightn ’ t suppose I was telling lies to her .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1081
sections.original: Perií , quot hic ipse annós vivet , cuius fílii tam diu vívont ?
sections.translation: I burst , I die ! ( Aloud .) How many years will he live himself whose sons live so long ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.1082
sections.original: Postríduo natus sum égo , mulier , quam Iúppiter ex Ope nátust .
sections.translation: Wench , I was born the day after Jupiter was born of Ops .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1083
sections.original: Si hic prídie natus forét quam ille est , hic habéret regnum in caélo .
sections.translation: If he had only been born the day before the other was , he would have had the realms of heaven .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1084
sections.original: Iam iám sat , amabo , est . sínite abeam , si póssum , viva a vóbis .
sections.translation: Now , now , prithee , no more do let me get away from you , if I can , alive .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1085
sections.original: Quin érgo abis , quando résponsumst ?
sections.translation: Why don ’ t you go then , as you have your answer ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.1085b
sections.original: Ibo átque illam huc addúcam ,
sections.translation: I ’ ll go , and I ’ ll bring her here , on whose behalf I am employed . Is there aught else you wish ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.1086b
sections.original: Ne mágis sim pulcher quám sum ,
sections.translation: May I never be more handsome than I am at present ; so much trouble do my good looks cause me .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1087b
sections.original: Quid hic núnc stas ? quin abis ?
sections.translation: Why do you stay now ? Why don ’ t you go ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.1087c
sections.original: Ábeo .
sections.translation: I ’ m going .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1088
sections.original: Atque ádeo , audin tu ? dicíto docte et cordáte , ut cor ei sáliat —
sections.translation: And tell her , too , do you hear , cleverly and correctly , what has passed .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1089
sections.original: Philocómasio dic , si ést istic , domum ut tránseat : hunc hic ésse .
sections.translation: If Philocomasium is there , tell her to pass through into our house ; that the Captain here .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1090
sections.original: Hic cúm mea era est , hinc clám nostrum hunc sermónem sublegérunt .
sections.translation: She is there with my mistress for , on the sly , they have been overhearing [*] this conversation .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1091
sections.original: Lepidé factumst : iam ex sérmone hoc gubernábunt doctius pórro .
sections.translation: ’ Twas cleverly done ; here after they will take their cue the more readily from this conversation .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1092
sections.original: Remoráre , abeo . —
sections.translation: You are delaying me . I ’ m off .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1092b
sections.original: Neque té remoror neque tángo neque te — táceo .
sections.translation: I ’ m not delaying you , nor touching you , nor [*] — I ’ m mum .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1093
sections.original: Iube máturare illam éxire huc . iam istíc rei praevortémur .
sections.translation: Bid her make haste to come out here ; we ’ ll give our first attention to this matter especially . ( MILPHIDIPPA goes into the house of PERIPLECOMENUS .)

sections.label: Section 4.3.1094
sections.original: Quid nunc mi es auctor ut faciam , Palaestrio ,
sections.translation: What do you advise me now to do , Palaestrio ,

sections.label: Section 4.3.1095
sections.original: de concubina ? nam nullo pacto potest
sections.translation: about my mistress ? For this lady can by no means be received into my house before I have sent the other away .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1097
sections.original: Quid me consultas quid agas ? dixi equidem tibi
sections.translation: Why consult me what you are to do ? So far as I am concerned , I have told you by what method that can be effected in the gentlest manner . The gold trinkets and female clothing with which you have furnished her , let her keep it all for herself : let her take it , be off , and carry it away :

sections.label: Section 4.3.1101
sections.original: dicasque tempus maxume esse , ut eat domum :
sections.translation: tell her that it is high time for her to go home ; say that her twin-sister and her mother are come , in company with whom she may go straight home .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1104
sections.original: Qui tu scis eas adesse ?
sections.translation: How do you know that they are here ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1104b
sections.original: Quia oculis meis
sections.translation: Because , with my own eyes , I ’ ve seen her sister here .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1105b
sections.original: Convenitne eam ?
sections.translation: Have you met her ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1106
sections.original: Convenit .
sections.translation: I have met her .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1106b
sections.original: Ecquid fortis visast ?
sections.translation: And did she seem a brisk wench ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1106c
sections.original: Omnia
sections.translation: You are wishing to have everything .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1107b
sections.original: Vbi matrem esse aiebat soror ?
sections.translation: Where did the sister say her mother was ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1108
sections.original: Cubare in navi lippam atque oculis turgidis
sections.translation: The captain that brought them told me that she was in bed , on board the ship , with sore and inflamed eyes .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1110
sections.original: is ad hos nauclerus hospitio devortitur .
sections.translation: This captain of the ship is lodging with them next door .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1111
sections.original: Quid is ? ecquid fortis ?
sections.translation: And he , too , is he a very fine fellow ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1111b
sections.original: Abi sis hinc , nam tu quidem
sections.translation: Away with you , if you please . What have you [*] to do with him ? You have your hands quite full enough with the women . Attend to this for the present .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1114b
sections.original: Istuc quod das consilium mihi ,
sections.translation: As to that advice you were giving me ,

sections.label: Section 4.3.1115
sections.original: te cum illa verba facere de ista re volo ;
sections.translation: I wish you to have a few words with her upon that subject . For , really , a conversation on that subject with her is more becoming [*] for you .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1117
sections.original: Qui potius quam tute adeas , tuam rem tute agas ?
sections.translation: What is more advisable than for you to go yourself , and transact your own concerns ? You must say that it is absolutely necessary for you to marry : that your relations are persuading , your friends are urging , you .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1120
sections.original: Itan tu censes ?
sections.translation: And do you think so ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1120b
sections.original: Quid ego ní ita censeam ?
sections.translation: Why shouldn ’ t I think so ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1121
sections.original: Ibo igitur intro . tu hic ante aedis interim
sections.translation: I ’ ll go in , then . Do you , in the mean time , keep watch here before the house , that when the other woman comes out you may call me out .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1123
sections.original: Tu modo istuc cura quod agis .
sections.translation: Do you only mind the business that you are upon .
